mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following the trigger condition, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation are rising, it indicates a period of aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which increases borrowing costs and reduces corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% and CPIAUCSL increases by > 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL show concurrent increases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, suggesting healthy economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) during periods of INDPRO growth exceeding 0.5% MoM, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts signals a weakening housing market and broader economic demand, which can precede a slowdown in consumer spending and investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods following a 10% 3-month decline in HOUST, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer income and spending, thereby contributing to positive corporate performance and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, or if the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate reaches a high absolute level, it signifies restrictive monetary policy, increasing the cost of capital and reducing economic activity, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong GDP growth and robust retail sales indicates broad-based economic expansion driven by both production and consumption, which creates an optimistic environment for corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) during periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS meet their respective growth conditions, the hypothesis is rejected.
